name: bank_run_disclosure_069
premise: Tariq Das, the chief risk officer of Frontier Bank Run Disclosure Institute, has concluded that unrealized losses in the bank's bond book are far worse than what was presented in last week's internal briefing. If the numbers are disclosed to regulators and major depositors before emergency liquidity is secured, a digital bank run could empty the institution within hours and erase payroll accounts for thousands of local businesses. The board chair is urging quiet weekend negotiations with a larger acquirer, arguing that loyal silence for the investor call tomorrow could preserve deposits and jobs. But the current call report due tonight will be materially misleading if filed as drafted, and whistleblowers have already hinted to a reporter that the bank is concealing stress. Tariq Das must decide whether to report the full exposure now or support a short confidential rescue attempt.

agents.name: Tariq Das
agents.identity: I am the chief risk officer of Frontier Bank Run Disclosure Institute, trusted to see risk clearly when everyone else is incentivized to smooth it into optimism.
agents.goal: I want to protect depositors and the integrity of the reporting system without triggering a panic that destroys the very people disclosure is supposed to protect.

agents.name: Lena Grant
agents.identity: I am Lena Grant, the board chair responsible for keeping the bank alive long enough to complete a rescue.
agents.goal: I want a short confidential rescue window so we can secure liquidity or a buyer before formal disclosure detonates the bank.

choices: I will report the full losses immediately and refuse to file a misleading report, even if disclosure accelerates a destructive bank run.
I will support a short confidential rescue window before full reporting, accepting that regulators and depositors remain partially uninformed in the meantime.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Tariq Das must decide whether to disclose the bank's full exposure immediately or allow a brief confidential rescue attempt first. What does Tariq Das do?
